commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat futures $6.25/bu (April 29 late, near 11-month high). USDA: only 30% winter wheat rated good/excellent (down from 45% YoY). US Great Plains drought and fertilizer constraints from Hormuz support prices. World Bank: fertilizer prices to increase 31% in 2026 (urea +60%).

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn $4.615/bu (April 29, four-week high). Acreage down to 95.3M from 99M due to fertilizer costs. Elevated diesel and transportation costs.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans $11.75/bu (April 28-29, down 0.18% daily). Soybean oil at historic highs — supported by biofuel demand as crude disruptions boost biodiesel. Cooking oil index at highest since June 2022.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: ICE Sugar #11 at $0.224/lb (April 25). Up 6.7% from pre-war. USDA forecasts sugar & sweets +6.7% in 2026.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.94/lb (Apr 10). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 but still elevated. Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.note: USDA projects food-at-home prices to increase 3.6% in 2026 — up from 3.1% in March forecast, nearly double original projection. Consumer sentiment dropped sharply in April; 12-month inflation expectations jumped to 4.7% from 3.8%. 45% of Americans cutting non-essential spending. 1/3 of globally traded fertilizer flows through Hormuz. 318M people face hunger globally (FAO).

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea surged to $650+/MT (+~50% in 3 weeks, some spot markets $800+). Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA projects 3.1% food price rise in 2026. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Critical Northern Hemisphere spring planting season underway.

context: Day 61 Late PT — Brent $111.12 (+39.2%). WTI $99.32 (+31.5%). Wheat $6.25 (+11.2%, near 11-month high). Corn $4.615 (+7.1%). Soybeans $11.75 (+16.1%). USDA: food-at-home prices to rise 3.1-3.6% in 2026. Consumer inflation expectations 4.7%. Fertilizer crisis: urea $640/MT (+35%); World Bank: fertilizer +31% in 2026, urea +60%. WFP: 45M more at risk of hunger if war extends past June. Gas $4.18 AAA (+33.5%). Bessent: Iran oil 'creaking.' GCC summit: Maximum Readiness. Hormuz Day 58. War Powers May 1 (2 days).
